summary: Connector framework — TLS 1.3 minimum for outbound connections
description_md:
The Connector Framework must enforce TLS 1.3 as the minimum protocol version for all outbound connections to external data sources to meet modern security standards and protect sensitive data in transit.

**Measurable Constraints**:

1. TLS 1.3 must be the minimum supported protocol; TLS 1.2 and below shall be disabled by default
2. Certificate validation must enforce strict hostname verification with a minimum key size of 2048 bits for RSA or 256 bits for ECC
3. All outbound connections must support SNI (Server Name Indication) and include automatic certificate pinning configuration
4. Connection timeout must be configurable with a default of 30 seconds, and retry logic should support exponential backoff up to 3 attempts
5. Security misconfiguration attempts (e.g., forcing TLS 1.2) must be logged to the audit log with severity level WARNING
